title: Why Can't I Sleep Like I Used To After 40? (A Hormone Expert Explains)
seo_title: Why Can't I Sleep Like I Used To After 40?
meta_description: Sleep problems after 40 are driven by falling progesterone, cortisol spikes, and estrogen changes. Learn what causes them and what actually helps you sleep again.
excerpt: Waking at 3 AM, lighter sleep, feeling unrested — these are not just stress. After 40, falling estrogen and progesterone directly disrupt the biology of deep sleep. Here is what is actually happening and what helps.
tags: sleep, hormones, perimenopause, magnesium, insomnia, women over 40, cortisol
author: Dr. Daniel Yadegar, MD
